# Node n13 Evidence — knowledge-lookup summon heuristic (adapt commands + workflow-planner)

## task
Add the `knowledge-lookup` summon heuristic to all four adapt command/SKILL files and to
`agents/workflow-planner.md`. Rename any residual `docs-lookup` references in those files
(none existed — this was a pure ADD).

## non_tdd_reason
**Glue / wiring** — prose authoring of a role-selection heuristic into planner and adapt
command files. No behavioral logic is added; the change is purely documentation/prose that
wires the renamed role into the shaping guidance. No natural failing unit test exists for
this category of change. Verified by grep assertions.

## write_set
- `commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/skills/kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md`
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`
- `agents/workflow-planner.md`

## what was changed

### Rename (`docs-lookup` → `knowledge-lookup`)
None of the five files contained `docs-lookup` or `DOCS_LOOKUP_MODEL` — this was a pure ADD.
(Those tokens were handled by earlier nodes n3–n12 which owned the actual role-token rename in
the files that carried them.)

### Addition — summon heuristic

**`commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`** (github):
Added a new `## Shaping guidance` section at the end of the file with the verbatim bullet:
> Author a `knowledge-lookup` node when the task depends on external library or API behavior,
> framework conventions, or open-web/expertise knowledge that cannot be confirmed from the local
> codebase alone. This mirrors the Phase 1 `knowledge-lookup` trigger.

The section header notes that the full shaping guidance lives in `agents/workflow-planner.md`
and that one heuristic is mirrored here. This lightly softens the earlier claim that shaping
guidance "lives exclusively in `agents/workflow-planner.md`" — n2 explicitly requires the
heuristic to be mirrored into the adapt commands, so the deviation is intentional and n13-owned.

**`plugins/kaola-workflow/skills/kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md`**:
Added the verbatim bullet after the existing "Choose the right implement role" shaping bullet
in the `## Shaping guidance` section (which already existed in SKILL.md).

**`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`**:
Same `## Shaping guidance` section added at end, byte-identical to github command
(no forge nouns in the heuristic — #309 cross-edition byte-identity).

**`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md`**:
Same `## Shaping guidance` section added at end, byte-identical to github and gitlab commands.

**`agents/workflow-planner.md`**:
Added after the "Choose the right implement role" bullet (~line 92):
> - **Use `knowledge-lookup`** when the task depends on external library/API behavior,
>   framework conventions, or open-web/expertise knowledge that cannot be confirmed locally:
>   author a `knowledge-lookup` node when the task depends on external library or API behavior,
>   framework conventions, or open-web/expertise knowledge that cannot be confirmed from the
>   local codebase alone. This mirrors the Phase 1 `knowledge-lookup` trigger.

## deviation from n2 spec (flagged per §4)
n2 §4 (Section 4) notes the deviation: task item 4's literal text ended "mirrors the Phase 1
`docs-lookup` trigger." Per the spec, n11 renames the Phase 1 trigger to `knowledge-lookup`,
so the cross-reference must say `knowledge-lookup` to point at the real post-rename token.
This node uses the `knowledge-lookup` form per the canonical spec (advisor-confirmed).

## placement note
The three thin command files (github/gitlab/gitea) had NO pre-existing shaping guidance section
— they delegate that to `agents/workflow-planner.md`. A new `## Shaping guidance` section was
created at the end of each to host the knowledge-lookup bullet. The heuristic wording is
byte-identical across all three command files (no forge nouns). The SKILL.md already had a
`## Shaping guidance` section with multiple bullets; the heuristic was appended there. The
workflow-planner.md bullet uses a lead-in ("Use `knowledge-lookup` when…") that matches the
existing "Choose the right implement role: Use `tdd-guide`… / Use `implementer`…" style in
that file, then delivers the verbatim sentence from n2 §4.

## verification_commands

### Verification 1: no residual docs-lookup
```
grep "docs-lookup" commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md \
  plugins/kaola-workflow/skills/kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md \
  plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md \
  plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md \
  agents/workflow-planner.md
```
Exit: 1 (no matches — PASS)

### Verification 2: knowledge-lookup heuristic present
```
grep "knowledge-lookup" commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md
```
Output:
```
- Author a `knowledge-lookup` node when the task depends on external library or API behavior, framework conventions, or open-web/expertise knowledge that cannot be confirmed from the local codebase alone. This mirrors the Phase 1 `knowledge-lookup` trigger.
```
Exit: 0 — PASS

### Suite
```
node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js
```
Output: `Workflow walkthrough simulation passed`
Exit: 0 — PASS

## before_result
Suite green before edit (no changes to behavioral scripts in this node's write-set).

## after_result
build-green — suite green after edit (`node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` exit 0).
All five files contain `knowledge-lookup` heuristic. Zero `docs-lookup` residual in write-set.
